skill.name: longbridge-technical
skill.description: Provides Longbridge-focused technical analysis workflows for candlestick patterns, Ichimoku, indicators, harmonic patterns, Elliott Wave, Chan Theory, Smart Money Concepts, and Turtle Trading signals.
skill.summary: Analyze market charts with Longbridge OHLCV data and structured technical frameworks.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many command, filesystem, environment, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and entropy hits. Review found the command hits are mainly Markdown examples and intended Longbridge CLI workflows, while weak-crypto, reconnaissance, env_access, and entropy hits are false positives from trading terminology, formatting, or multilingual text. The remaining real risk is moderate because the skill can run CLI commands, install third-party Python packages, write temp files, and access account-related Longbridge data when the user authorizes it.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands and Package Installation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands for market data and help output, and to install third-party Python packages for Chan Theory, Smart Money Concepts, and Elliott Wave support. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes local commands and depends on external packages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain direct command examples and dependency installation instructions. The commands match the skill purpose and do not show malicious payloads, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Account and Portfolio Data Access Through Longbridge CLI
medium_findings.description: The Turtle Trading workflow can call Longbridge assets, positions, orders, and watchlist commands to calculate unit sizing, exits, and scans. This may expose account value, holdings, order history, and watchlist data to the assistant context when the user is logged in.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists account, positions, orders, and watchlist commands and describes when to call them. The same file also says not to read holdings without user authorization, which lowers maliciousness but not privacy impact.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, env_access, and entropy alerts are not supported by malicious context. They map to trading words such as index codes and market environment, Markdown tables, examples, and multilingual documentation rather than crypto routines, host reconnaissance, environment variable access, or obfuscated payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found market-analysis terminology and documentation structure, not dangerous implementation code. Confidence is high, though the full static list was large and grouped for review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown instructions ask the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands and Python scripts. The commands are tied to market-data retrieval and analysis, but marketplace users should understand that local command execution may occur.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct command examples are present across the cited files. They are expected for the Longbridge workflow and not evidence of command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Temporary File and Device Redirection Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The Elliott Wave and Turtle references instruct use of /tmp files and stderr redirection to handle CLI output. This is a normal data-processing pattern, but it is still filesystem interaction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly show temp-file and redirection guidance. The usage is limited to parsing CLI output and does not show destructive file operations.

content.value_statement: Manual chart analysis is slow and inconsistent across technical frameworks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Longbridge workflows for repeatable technical analysis.
actual_capabilities: Fetches Longbridge OHLCV data through CLI or MCP fallback instructions.
Guides candlestick, Ichimoku, RSI, MACD, EMA, Bollinger, ADX, and OBV analysis.
Supports harmonic, Elliott Wave, Chan Theory, and Smart Money Concepts workflows.
Calculates Turtle Trading breakout signals, ATR values, stop levels, and unit sizing.
Provides bilingual or trilingual output guidance for Chinese and English users.
Defines error handling for missing data, missing packages, login failures, and unsupported symbols.
limitations: Requires reliable Longbridge market data and enough historical candles for each framework.
Some workflows depend on optional Python packages that may not be installed.
Trading signals are analytical references and are not investment advice.
Several advanced references mention helper scripts or files that are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Screen Breakout Candidates
use_cases.description: Scan watchlists or selected symbols for Turtle Trading entry, exit, stop, and position-size references.

use_cases.title: Validate Chart Patterns
use_cases.description: Compare candlestick, Ichimoku, harmonic, Elliott Wave, and indicator signals before writing a market note.

use_cases.title: Explain Technical Signals
use_cases.description: Turn complex technical frameworks into structured explanations with caveats and clear data-source attribution.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Indicator Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge technical indicators to analyze AAPL.US. Explain RSI, MACD, EMA, Bollinger Bands, and the final signal in plain English.

prompt_templates.title: Candlestick and Ichimoku Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 700.HK with candlestick patterns and Ichimoku Cloud. Show the main signal, supporting evidence, and any conflicting indicators.

prompt_templates.title: Turtle Position Sizing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Turtle Trading workflow for NVDA.US. Calculate S1 and S2 signals, ATR N value, stop level, add-on level, and one Unit size.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Framework Technical Memo
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a technical memo for 600519.SH using Elliott Wave, harmonic patterns, Smart Money Concepts, and classic indicators. Include caveats and invalidation levels.

output_examples.input: Use technical indicators to review AAPL.US.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes current indicator values, gives each indicator a bullish, bearish, or neutral vote, and explains the composite result with Longbridge as the data source.

output_examples.input: Scan my watchlist for Turtle Trading signals.
output_examples.output: The response lists symbols with breakout, exit, or no-signal status, then adds stop levels, ATR context, liquidity warnings, and position-size notes when account data is available.

output_examples.input: Analyze 700.HK with Elliott Wave and Ichimoku.
output_examples.output: The response describes the likely wave stage, key Fibonacci zones, Ichimoku confirmation or conflict, and the price level that would invalidate the view.

best_practices: Confirm the symbol format and available Longbridge CLI flags before analysis.
Use enough historical candles for the selected framework and state data limits clearly.
Ask for user authorization before reading account, positions, orders, or watchlist data.
anti_patterns: Do not present technical signals as guaranteed price predictions.
Do not calculate unit sizing from account data unless the user authorizes access.
Do not ignore missing data, missing packages, unsupported symbols, or conflicting signals.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis workflows and signal explanations. It does not submit orders.

faq.question: Does it need a Longbridge login?
faq.answer: Basic market data may work without login. Account, positions, orders, and watchlist workflows require an authorized Longbridge session.

faq.question: Which markets are covered?
faq.answer: The instructions cover Hong Kong, United States, A-share, and Singapore stock markets.

faq.question: Can it work without optional Python packages?
faq.answer: Some frameworks include fallback logic, but Chan Theory and Smart Money Concepts are more accurate with their optional packages installed.

faq.question: Is this investment advice?
faq.answer: No. Outputs are technical references based on historical data and should include clear risk caveats.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can run CLI commands and read account-related data when authorized. No malicious behavior was confirmed.
